data.title: Bitcoin volatile despite increased odds of rate cut after weak jobs data
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) price remained volatile on Friday, erasing early gains as investors still remain cautious following a weaker-than-expected jobs report.

August’s nonfarm payrolls report showed the economy added only 22,000 jobs—well below the 75,000 consensus forecast and trailing July’s 79,000 increase. At the same time, the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.3% from 4.2% in July, matching expectations.

Bitcoin prices climbed to $113,000 following the report’s release. But the rally was short-lived as it soon cooled down to $110,500. Ethereum (ETH-USD) and XRP (NASDAQ:XRPI) fell 1.1% and 0.7%, respectively, over the same period.

However, all of them turned green in the afternoon trading.

Historically, Bitcoin has reacted positively to signs of lower interest rates, as it could be a tailwind for risk assets, and Bitcoin could be among the primary beneficiaries.

The odds of a Fed rate cut increased following today’s data.

“Bitcoin is well-positioned to outperform in the next round of monetary policy easing, especially after a period of cooled sentiment,” noted a recent Seeking Alpha analyst, adding that liquidity from monetary easing is the main driver of asset prices.

Other crypto-related stocks, including MicroStrategy (MSTR) and MARA Holdings (MARA), were up in the afternoon. Meanwhile, Coinbase Global (COIN) was trading over 2% lower, while HIVE Digital (HIVE) and Hut 8 (HUT) were down over 1%.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Apple event, Oracle earnings, and Klarna leads an IPO blitz
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Sunday - September 7

  • All day - Eight OPEC+ nations meet online to assess oil markets.

 

Monday - September 8

  • Volatility watch - Lucid Group (LCID) and aTyr Pharma (ATYR) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Apex Critical Metals (OTCQB:APXCF), Mineralys Therapeutics (MLYS), and Golconda Gold (OTCQB:GGGOF). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include ARS Pharmaceuticals (SPRY), Next Technology Holding (NXTT), and SafeSpace Global (OTCPK:SSGC)
  • IPO watch - Klarna Group (KLAR) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading during the week. The Swedish fintech giant aims to raise up to $1.27 billion to value the company at up to $14 billion. Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR), Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB), Gemini Space Station (GEMI), Legence Corp. (LGN), and Via Transportation (VIA) are all expected to start trading as well. The quiet periods expire on Bullish (BLSH), Miami International (MIAX), and Magnitude International (MAGH) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Casey's General Stores (CASY) and Planet Labs (PL).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include FedEx (FDX), Ross Stores (ROST), and Travelers (TRV).
  • All week - The three-day Goldman Sachs Communacopia + Technology Conference will begin in San Francisco. Notable companies appearing include Mastercard (MA), Uber (UBER), AMD (AMD), Snowflake (SNOW), News Corp (NWSA), Seagate Technology (STX), ASML (ASML), Autodesk (ADSK), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), and Coinbase (COIN). Other high-profile conferences include the Bank of America Media, Communications & Entertainment Conference, the Cantor Fitzgerald Global Healthcare Conference, the Citi Global TMT Conference, the Baird Healthcare Conference, the Lake Street Best Ideas Growth Conference, and the Wolfe Research TMT Conference.
  • Data reports - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
  • All day - McDonald's (MCD) is launching its revived Extra Value Meals across U.S. locations in what could be a major pricing shot in the fast-food restaurant sector.
  • All day - IDEAYA Biosciences (IDYA) will hold an in-person R&D Day in conjunction with the company's 10-Year anniversary.
  • 3:25 p.m. NVIDIA (NVDA) will present at the Goldman Sachs Communacopia + Technology Conference.

 

Tuesday - September 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), SailPoint (SAIL), and Rubrik (RBRK). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for GameStop (GME) after it reports.
  • All day - Shareholders with SpartanNash (SPTN) will vote on the proposed merger with New Mackinac HoldCo, Inc., an affiliate of C&S Wholesale Grocers, LLC.
  • All day - Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) will hold its "Awe Dropping" event to showcase major hardware announcements, headlined by the ultra-thin iPhone 17 Air and new advances in the Apple Watch line.
  • All day - Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF), Qualcomm (QCOM), Google (GOOG), Cerence (CRNC) and Magna (MGA) are some of the notable companies participating at the IAA Mobility event in Munich.
  • 12:30 p.m. Meta (META) CFO Susan Li will participate in the Goldman Sachs Communacopia and Technology Conference.

 

Wednesday - September 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Chewy (CHWY) and Oxford Industries (OXM).
  • All day - Tapestry (TPR) will hold its Investor Day. The event will highlight the company's long-term strategic growth initiatives and financial outlook, featuring a series of presentations as well as a question and answer session with members of the senior leadership team.
  • All day - Shareholders with TaskUs (TASK) will vote on the Blackstone (BX) acquisition offer. Shares of TaskUs traded at a 16% premium to the deal price at the time of publication.

 

Thursday - September 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Adobe (ADBE) and Kroger (KR). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Lovesac (LOVE) and Vera Bradley (VRA) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - Corporate events include Freshworks' (FRSH) Investor Day, Arista Networks' (ANET) Analyst Day, and Box's (BOX) annual content and AI conference.
  • All day - The OPEC monthly oil market report will be released.
  • 8:15 a.m. The European Central Bank will release its monetary policy statement. ECB officials will also hold a press conference
  • 8:30 a.m. The Consumer Price Index report for August will be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to hold steady at +3.1%.

 

Friday - September 12

  • All day - Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) will release the Borderlands 4 videogame for PlayStation 5, Windows, and Xbox Series X/S, with a Nintendo Switch 2 release following on October 3.

 

Seeking Alpha editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: Chicago Fed's Goolsbee awaits inflation data before backing rate path
data.description:
Even after a poor August jobs report earlier on Friday, Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago Austan Goolsbee said he is holding off on taking a position for the central bank's upcoming meeting until fresh inflation figures arrive next week.

Speaking in an interview with Bloomberg Television, Goolsbee said "the more mild numbers we get on inflation, the better I’ll feel about just focusing on the labor market.” The August Consumer Price Index due out on Sept. 11.

“But in the last inflation reports, we also had this uptick in inflation coming from services, so I think we want to make sure that that’s more of a blip and not a more ominous indicator,” the Chicago Fed chief added. 

The jobs report released earlier Friday showed further signs of weakness in the labor market, with revisions showing total net job losses for June.

Goolsbee said that layoffs and vacancy rates reflect "pretty stable full employment, he told Bloomberg. Still, August employment growth "definitely" trailed what's required to maintain a steady unemployment rate, he added.

data.title: TMX Group reports higher trading activities in March
data.description:
• Canada-based TMX Group (TSX:X:CA) said on Friday that its marketplaces reported August trading activities worth $300.14B, almost flat from the prior month and 21.3% from the same period a year ago.
  • The financial exchange operator said its August trading volume came at 12.2B, compared to 12.97B in July and 10.26B in August 2024.
  • The company reported 23.19M transactions for the month, down from 23.87M in the prior month and 20.97M a year ago.
  • Average daily transaction volume for August 2025 was 610.0M, with the average daily transaction value standing at $15B.

data.title: CVS Caremark facing class action suit for dropping coverage of Lilly's Zepbound
data.description:
A class action lawsuit has been filed against CVS Caremark (NYSE:CVS), the pharmacy benefit manager for health insurer Aetna, after it dropped Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) weight loss med Zepbound (tirzepatide) from its covered formulary list and replaced it with Novo Nordisk's Wegovy (semaglutide).

The suit, filed Sept. 3 in federal court for the Southern District of New York, argues that the two drugs cannot be substituted.

"Because of their different methods of action, different clinical outcomes, and different side effects for individual patients, Zepbound and Wegovy are not clinically interchangeable," according to the complaint. “And as studies have proven, Zepbound is more effective than Wegovy in achieving weight loss, and only Zepbound has been proven effective and approved by the FDA for treating sleep apnea in patients with obesity."

CVS spokesman David Whitrap told Seeking Alpha that the suit "is without merit."

He added that a study based on real-world data released on Aug. 31 by Novo showed that Wegovy led to a 50% decrease in serious heart issues such as stroke compared to Zepbound. 

"Wegovy and Zepbound are two clinically similar products, and by forcing the drug manufacturers to compete with one another on price, CVS Caremark was able to unlock significant cost savings for the employers and unions that hire us to manage their pharmacy benefits," Whitrap said. "In addition to increasing affordability and access to this therapy class immediately, we anticipate that our formulary decision will encourage both Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk to more aggressively compete with one another in the future and continue to lower the U.S. prices for their products."

data.title: IGM Financial reports record AUM of $292.6B in August 2025
data.description:
• IGM Financial (TSX:IGM:CA) reported record-high total assets under management and advisement of $292.6B, up 12.4% Y/Y and 1.6% M/M.
  • Total net inflows for August 2025 were $434M vs $142M in August 2024.
  • IG Wealth Management reached record-high assets under advisement of $151B.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tech Voices: Tesla on xAI investment, EU vs. Google, Qualcomm on Intel
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shareholders will have a chance to vote in November on whether the EV company should invest in AI developer xAI, which is majority owned by Tesla CEO Elon Musk. 

According to a proposal appearing in Tesla's latest proxy statement, shareholders will vote on whether to authorize Tesla's board to make an investment in xAI  "in an amount and form deemed appropriate by the Board, to capitalize on the synergies between the two companies and strengthen Tesla’s position as a leader in AI, robotics, and energy."

Tesla shareholders are scheduled to vote on the measure during their annual meeting on Nov. 6.

Known largely for its AI chatbot Grok, xAI also owns X, formerly known as Twitter. SpaceX (SPACEX), another Musk company, was part of a $5 billion fundraising round for xAI announced in June, committing $2 billion. 

  • The European Commission is fining Alphabet's Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) 2.95 billion euros, or around $3.45 billion, for violating EU antitrust rules with its adtech business.

"Today’s decision shows that Google abused its dominant position in adtech harming publishers, advertisers, and consumers. This behaviour is illegal under EU antitrust rules. Google must now come forward with a serious remedy to address its conflicts of interest, and if it fails to do so, we will not hesitate to impose strong remedies," said EC official Teresa Ribera, in a statement. 

"The Commission has already signaled its preliminary view that only the divestment by Google of part of its services would address the situation of inherent conflicts of interest, but it first wishes to hear and assess Google's proposal," the EC added. 

  • Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon said that Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) needs to improve its production technology in order for his company to consider using it as a supplier.

“Intel is not an option today,” Amon told Bloomberg TV on Friday. “We would like Intel to be an option.”

Qualcomm currently uses Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Samsung Electronics as suppliers.

data.title: Imperial Equities appoints Adam Chadi as COO
data.description:
• Imperial Equities (TSXV:IEI:CA) announced on Friday that it has appointed Adam Chadi, as the company's chief operating officer, effective September 1, 2025.
  • "I am pleased to welcome Adam to the senior leadership team of our growing company," said Sine Chadi, CEO. "With our strong investment portfolio and our ambitious plans for future growth, Adam's legal background and operational knowledge are poised to help us plan for the future focus."  
  • IEI:CA is +5.47% to $3.85
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crown Holdings upgraded to Buy at Truist as recent selloff presents attractive entry point
data.description:
Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) +1.4% in Friday's trading as Truist Securities upgraded the maker of metal beverage and food cans to Buy from Hold with a $118 price target, citing the stock’s attractive entry point due to recent notable weakness and North America volume growth prospects in 2026.

Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) shares have declined nearly 8% since August 28 despite a lack of any specific actionable company news, and the stock now trades at ~8.1x 2026 EBITDA compared to a 9.5x average over the last five years, which Truist analyst Michael Roxland said presents an investment opportunity.

Despite recent modest volume softness, Roxland believes Crown (CCK) will meet its Q3 earnings guidance of $1.95-$2.05/share, partly due to strong operations and productivity, and the company also should be able to meet or exceed its full-year EPS outlook of $7.10-7.50, partly as it only raised guidance by $0.50 in Q2 while beating its own guidance for the first two quarters by $0.72 combined, implying continued conservatism.

For 2026, the analyst thinks Crown (CCK) could benefit from some new business wins in North
America, potentially leading to above market growth and ultimately contributing to share price outperformance.
